behavior_summary: Provides the "U8P_OVEN::look" (examine) handler for the oven object: when the player inspects the oven the script shows a short bark reading "oven ".
The process contains no branching, menus, external calls, or flag manipulation — it is a minimal descriptive response for a static object.
After emitting the bark the process appears to suspend/terminate, leaving no persistent state changes.
notable_control_flow: U8P_OVEN::look is executed as a process-type handler (process).
Execution flow: entry -> emit single Bark "oven " -> suspend/terminate the U8P_OVEN::look process.
No conditional branches, loops, or calls to other scripts or engine functions are present.
open_questions: The bark text contains a trailing space ("oven "): is that intentional (formatting/concatenation placeholder) or an artifact?

What in-game event exactly triggers this process (explicit player 'U8P_OVEN::look' action vs. fast-area entry) in this asset's placement?
